title: Issues, Reviews, Pull Requests, and Team Workflow · History Brief: Review Feedback
summary: Move a change from issue to review to integration with traceable decisions, bounded branch scope, and respectful collaboration. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git review feedback increment in a team contribution and review protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Review behavior, risks, tests, readability, and security with precise evidence while separating required changes from suggestions.
Prepare a focused review range, description, changed-case evidence, migration notes, and explicit reviewer questions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Review Feedback
steps.instruction:
theory case — Issue 5 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Review Feedback contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for review behavior, risks, tests, readability, and security with precise evidence while separating required changes from suggestions.

content.text: Issue 5 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Review Feedback condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Pro Git, Second Edition — Controls cumulative conceptual sequence from setup through internals, collaboration, tooling, and distributed workflows.

options.text: Code review is primarily a style-correction process.

Review behavior, risks, tests, readability, and security with precise evidence while separating required changes from suggestions. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

steps.title: Bound Review Feedback
steps.instruction:
theory case — Issue 6 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Review Feedback application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for review behavior, risks, tests, readability, and security with precise evidence while separating required changes from suggestions.

content.text: Review behavior, risks, tests, readability, and security with precise evidence while separating required changes from suggestions. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Issue 5 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy. A defensible solution applies Review Feedback, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a team contribution and review protocol.

content.code:
# Evidence: changed Git case 4
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Code review is primarily a style-correction process. This fails the Review Feedback contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Review Feedback evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-review-feedback-1y5qm1" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Review Feedback into observable performance evidence.

steps.title: Repair the Review Feedback defect
steps.instruction:
theory case — Issue 4 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.

A teammate acts on this belief: “Code review is primarily a style-correction process.” Which repair addresses the cause?

steps.title: Defend the Review Feedback model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Review Feedback: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Issue 6 requires one behavior and accessibility regression. A pull request contains five unrelated cleanup commits; define review range, split scope, required evidence, and authorized merge policy.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Review behavior, risks, tests, readability, and security with precise evidence while separating required changes from suggestions.
